CHANGELOG.html.title: Changelog | Chobitech.DemoKit
CHANGELOG.html.summary: Changelog All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file. [1.0.0] - 2026-04-16 Added Initial release of Chobitech.DemoKit. Support for Asynchronous (Task-based) and Coroutine-based demo logic. Real-time logging system with rich-text support. ScriptableObject-driven configuration system. Online documentations and sample scenes.

api/Chobitech.DemoKit.AsyncDemoBase.html.summary: Class AsyncDemoBase Namespace Chobitech.DemoKit Assembly Chobitech.DemoKit.Internal.dll Base class for asynchronous demonstrations using Task and CancellationToken. Ideal for complex asynchronous logic, awaiting multiple operations, or integrating with Task-based APIs. This class inherits from DemoBase to provide metadata and logging functionality. public abstract class AsyncDemoBase : DemoBase Inheritance object Object Component Behaviour MonoBehaviour DemoBase DemoKitExtensions.RunIfUnityObjectIsNotNull<T, U>(object, U, Func<U, T>, T) Methods DemoProcessAsync(CancellationToken) Defines the main execution logic of the asynchronous demonstration. This method is invoked by the DemoOrchestrator. Implementations should respect the provided CancellationToken to ensure responsive cancellation. public abstract Task DemoProcessAsync(CancellationToken token) Parameters token CancellationToken A token used to monitor for cancellation requests from the orchestrator or system. Returns Task A Task representing the ongoing asynchronous operation. OnDemoCanceled(CancellationToken) Triggered when the asynchronous demonstration process is canceled via the CancellationToken. Override this method to perform specific cleanup, UI resets, or custom logging upon cancellation. public virtual void OnDemoCanceled(CancellationToken token) Parameters token CancellationToken The cancellation token that triggered this state.

api/Chobitech.DemoKit.CoroutineDemoBase..summary: Class CoroutineDemoBase Namespace Chobitech.DemoKit Assembly Chobitech.DemoKit.Internal.dll Base class for demonstrations using Unity Coroutines (IEnumerator). This method is started and managed by the DemoOrchestrator using the Unity Coroutine system. public abstract IEnumerator DemoRoutine() Returns IEnumerator An IEnumerator used by the Unity Coroutine engine. OnDemoCanceled() Triggered when the coroutine demonstration is forcibly stopped (canceled) by the orchestrator. Override this method to handle specific cleanup, UI resets, or state restoration for canceled coroutines. public virtual void OnDemoCanceled()

api/Chobitech.DemoKit.CustomColorTag.htm.title: Class CustomColorTag | Chobitech.DemoKit
api/Chobitech.DemoKit.CustomColorTag.htm.summary: Class CustomColorTag Namespace Chobitech.DemoKit Assembly Chobitech.DemoKit.Internal.dll A ScriptableObject that defines a custom color mapping for text tags. Used to associate a specific string tag with a color for rich text processing within the DemoKit log system. DemoKitExtensions.RunIfUnityObjectIsNotNull<T, U>(object, U, Func<U, T>, T) Fields color The color associated with this tag. public Color color Field Value Color tag The string identifier for the tag (e.g., "highlight", "warning"). This tag is used in logs to apply the color defined in this asset. public string tag Field Value string Properties Hex Gets the hexadecimal string representation of the color. The value is cached after the first access to optimize performance during log generation. public string Hex { get; } Property Value string Methods GetTaggedText(string) Wraps the provided content string with the defined tag in a pseudo-markup format. public string GetTaggedText(string content) Parameters content string The text content to be wrapped. Returns string A string formatted as [tag]content[/tag].

api/Chobitech.DemoKit.CustomColorTagExte.title: Class CustomColorTagExtensions | Chobitech.DemoKit
api/Chobitech.DemoKit.CustomColorTagExte.summary: Class CustomColorTagExtensions Namespace Chobitech.DemoKit Assembly Chobitech.DemoKit.Internal.dll Provides extension methods for collections of CustomColorTag to facilitate color processing. public static class CustomColorTagExtensions Inheritance object CustomColorTagExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods GetTagAndHexMap(IEnumerable<CustomColorTag>) Converts a collection of CustomColorTag into a dictionary mapping tags to their hex color strings. This dictionary is used for fast lookup during the rich text conversion process. public static Dictionary<string, string> GetTagAndHexMap(this IEnumerable<CustomColorTag> tags) Parameters tags IEnumerable<CustomColorTag> The collection of custom color tags. Returns Dictionary<string, string> A dictionary where the key is the tag string and the value is the hex color string.

Returns a default name if the metadata is missing. public string DemoName { get; } Property Value string Description Gets the description text of the demonstration. public string Description { get; } Property Value string IndividualDemoInfo Gets the metadata associated with this specific demonstration. public IndividualDemoInfo IndividualDemoInfo { get; } Property Value IndividualDemoInfo Methods AddLog(string) Appends a message to the internal log buffer and updates the UI. public void AddLog(string log) Parameters log string The text message to add to the log. AddLogLn(string) Appends a message followed by a newline character to the internal log buffer and updates the UI. public void AddLogLn(string log) Parameters log string The text message to add to the log. AddLogLnWithColorTag(string, string) Appends a message wrapped in a custom color tag, followed by a newline, to the log buffer. public void AddLogLnWithColorTag(string tag, string log) Parameters tag string The identifier of the custom tag. log string The text message to add to the log. AddLogWithColorTag(string, string) Appends a message wrapped in a custom color tag to the log buffer. Custom tags are defined in the GlobalDemoInfo asset. public void AddLogWithColorTag(string tag, string log) Parameters tag string The identifier of the custom tag. log string The text message to add to the log. ApplyStoredLog() Sends the current content of the internal log buffer to the UI display via the orchestrator. public void ApplyStoredLog() Awake() Initializes the demo component and performs a validation check on the metadata. protected virtual void Awake() ClearLog() Clears the internal log buffer and updates the UI display to reflect the empty state. public void ClearLog() OnDemoCompleted() Called when the demonstration process completes successfully without being canceled. Override this to perform specific actions upon successful demo completion. public virtual void OnDemoCompleted()

api/Chobitech.DemoKit.DemoKitExtensions..title: Class DemoKitExtensions | Chobitech.DemoKit
api/Chobitech.DemoKit.DemoKitExtensions..summary: Class DemoKitExtensions Namespace Chobitech.DemoKit Assembly Chobitech.DemoKit.Internal.dll Provides a set of static extension methods used throughout the DemoKit system for color conversion, safe Unity Object execution, and functional programming patterns. public static class DemoKitExtensions Inheritance object DemoKitExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Also<T>(T, UnityAction<T>) Calls a specified action on the object and returns the object itself. Useful for performing side effects or configuring an object in a chain. public static T Also<T>(this T t, UnityAction<T> action) Parameters t T The object instance. action UnityAction<T> The action to perform on the object. Returns T The original object instance. Type Parameters T The type of the object. Let<T, R>(T, Func<T, R>) Calls a specified function on the object and returns the result. Useful for transforming values or scoping operations. public static R Let<T, R>(this T t, Func<T, R> func) Parameters t T The object instance. func Func<T, R> The function to apply to the object. Returns R The result of func, or default(R) if func is null. Type Parameters T The type of the object. R The type of the result. RunIfUnityObjectIsNotNull<U>(object, U, UnityAction<U>) Safely executes an action that takes a Unity Object, provided the object is not null. Logs a warning using DemoKitLog if the Unity Object is null. public static void RunIfUnityObjectIsNotNull<U>(this object obj, U unityObj, UnityAction<U> action) where U : Object Parameters obj object The calling instance (ignored, used for extension scope). unityObj U The Unity Object to validate. action UnityAction<U> The action to execute if the object is valid. Type Parameters U The type of the Unity Object (must inherit from UnityEngine.Object). RunIfUnityObjectIsNotNull<T, U>(object, U, Func<U, T>, T) Safely executes a function that takes a Unity Object, provided the object is not null. Logs a warning using DemoKitLog if the Unity Object is null. public static T RunIfUnityObjectIsNotNull<T, U>(this object obj, U unityObj, Func<U, T> func, T defVal = default) where U : Object Parameters obj object The calling instance (ignored, used for extension scope). unityObj U The Unity Object to validate. func Func<U, T> The function to execute if the object is valid. defVal T The default value to return if the object is null or destroyed. Returns T The result of the function if unityObj is valid; otherwise, defVal. Type Parameters T The return type of the function. U The type of the Unity Object (must inherit from UnityEngine.Object). ToHex(int) Converts an integer to a lowercase two-digit hexadecimal string. public static string ToHex(this int i) Parameters i int The integer value to convert. Returns string A hexadecimal string (e.g., "ff"). ToHex(float) Converts a normalized float value to a two-digit hexadecimal string. public static string ToHex(this float f) Parameters f float The normalized float value. Returns string A hexadecimal string representation of the float. ToHexInt(float) Converts a normalized float value (0.0 to 1.0) to a byte-range integer (0 to 255). public static int ToHexInt(this float f) Parameters f float The normalized float value. Returns int An integer clamped between 0 and 255. ToHexString(Color) Converts a Unity Color to a hexadecimal color string with a leading hash. public static string ToHexString(this Color c) Parameters c Color The color to convert. Returns string A string in the format #RRGGBBAA.

Info(object, Object) Logs a standard information message to the Unity Console. This method is only active in the Unity Editor. Warning(object, Object) Logs a warning message to the Unity Console. This method is only active in the Unity Editor. api/Chobitech.DemoKit.html.title: Namespace Chobitech.DemoKit | Chobitech.DemoKit
api/Chobitech.DemoKit.html.summary: Namespace Chobitech.DemoKit Classes AsyncDemoBase Base class for asynchronous demonstrations using Task and CancellationToken. Ideal for complex asynchronous logic, awaiting multiple operations, or integrating with Task-based APIs. This class inherits from DemoBase to provide metadata and logging functionality. CoroutineDemoBase Base class for demonstrations using Unity Coroutines (IEnumerator). Suitable for tasks that are tightly integrated with the Unity lifecycle or for users who prefer the standard StartCoroutine pattern. Inherits from DemoBase to provide metadata and logging functionality. CustomColorTag A ScriptableObject that defines a custom color mapping for text tags. Used to associate a specific string tag with a color for rich text processing within the DemoKit log system. CustomColorTagExtensions Provides extension methods for collections of CustomColorTag to facilitate color processing. DemoBase Base class for all demonstration components. Provides common functionality for metadata management, logging, and lifecycle hooks. This class is inherited by AsyncDemoBase and CoroutineDemoBase. DemoKitExtensions Provides a set of static extension methods used throughout the DemoKit system for color conversion, safe Unity Object execution, and functional programming patterns. DemoKitLog Provides a standardized logging interface for the DemoKit package. All logs are prefixed with a rich text tag and are conditionally compiled to be editor-only. DemoKitUtils Provides utility constants and shared properties for the DemoKit system. DemoKitUtils.DefaultValues Contains default strings and file names used during the demo generation process. DemoKitUtils.PresetGUID Contains constant GUID strings for identifying specific assets and folders within the project. DemoOrchestrator The central controller for the DemoKit system. It manages the lifecycle of demonstrations, coordinates between UI components (Header, Footer, Log, Description), and handles asynchronous execution and cancellation of demo processes. GlobalDemoInfo A ScriptableObject that stores global configuration and metadata for the entire DemoKit system. It centralizes information like titles, icons, and shared rich text color tags used by all demo scenes. IndividualDemoInfo A ScriptableObject that holds specific metadata for an individual demonstration. It stores the title and a detailed description to be displayed within the DemoKit UI system. Each DemoBase component requires a reference to this asset.

getting-started.html.title: Getting Started | Chobitech.DemoKit
getting-started.html.summary: Getting Started This guide will help you set up Chobitech.DemoKit and create your first demo. Getting Started Prerequisites Installation Installation via Asset Store via Unity Package Running Demo with Chobitech.DemoKit Demo Creation Workflow Open the Demo Setup Window 0. Import Required Assets 1. Initial Setup 2. Implement Your Demo 3. Distribution Setup References Prerequisites Unity 2022.3 or later TextMeshPro (Essential Resources must be imported) Installation Installation via Asset Store chobitech Asset Store page: Please add Chobitech.DemoKit to My Assets and import your project. via Unity Package Download the latest .unitypackage from GitHub Release. Drag and drop the downloaded file into your Unity Project, or import it via the menu [Assets] > [Import Package] > [Custom Package] and select the .unitypackage file. Running Demo with Chobitech.DemoKit Callouts of DemoMainScene Global Title: Displays the name of the overall demo project. Global Description: Provides a general overview and instructions for the entire demo toolkit. Individual Demo Info: Shows the title and specific details of the currently selected individual demo. The side panel containing both Global and Individual descriptions can be toggled via the icon button in the top-left corner. Demo Controller: Contains the dropdown for selecting demos and the Run/Stop toggle button. Log Console: Displays real-time logs and execution status for the active demo. This panel can be toggled via the "Open/Close Log" button in the top-right corner. Demo Creation Workflow Open the Demo Setup Window Demo Setup Window In Chobitech.DemoKit, you can setup your demo with the setup wizard on the Demo Setup Window. Show Demo Setup Window with following ways: Dedicated Window: [Window] > [Chobitech] > [DemoKit] > [Demo Setup Window] Project Settings: [Project Settings] > [Chobitech] > [DemoKit Setup] 0. Import Required Assets Import Required Assets Section After the setup window is displayed, see [Import Required Assets] section at first. Chobitech.DemoKit requires TMP Essential Resources included in TextMeshPro to display texts on the demo main scene, so it needs to import TMP Essential Resources. If you did not import the resources yet, press the "Import TMP Essential Resources" button in the setup window and import TMP Essential Resources. Or, if you already imported the resources, skip this section. Important The setup wizard requires you to complete importing TMP Essential Resources before proceeding. 1. Initial Setup Initial Setup Section Next, initialize your demo in this section with the following three steps: Step 1: Input the name of your demo This name is set to GlobalDemoInfo asset described later. Step 2: Select the workspace folder The selected folder works as the workspace of your demo. Step 3: Setup Required Assets To copy the requires assets, DemoMainScene and GlobalDemoInfo, press the "Setup Requires Assets" button. When the button is pressed, the setup wizard copies the required assets to the workspace selected in Step 2. Note Required Assets DemoMainScene: The main scene of your demo. GlobalDemoInfo: The ScriptableObject holds the global demo information. In coping the assets, GlobalDemoInfo will be set to DemoMainScene automatically. Note "Auto-add EventSystem" Checkbox When checked, the setup wizard automatically adds a pre-configured EventSystem to your DemoMainScene. If you want to add a customized EventSystem, uncheck this. Important Files in Workspace Folder When generating the distribution files in Distribution Setup section, the files in the workspace folder are only included. For more detail, see the "3. Distribution Setup" section. 2. Implement Your Demo Open the DemoMainScene copied to your workspace and start implementing your demo. Chobitech.DemoKit allows you to manage and switch between multiple demos within a single scene. Caution Protect System Objects To ensure stability, do not edit the preset objects (e.g., Orchestrator, Header, Footer, etc.) within the DemoMainScene. These are essential for the DemoKit core logic. Demo implementation workflow is below: (A) Configure IndividualDemoInfo IndividualDemoInfo is the information holder for your demo. By attaching an IndividualDemoInfo asset to your demo objects, the demo information will be automatically displayed in the DemoMainScene UI. Note To generate a new IndividualDemoInfo asset, select the menu: [Assets] > [Create] > [Chobitech] > [DemoKit] > [Individual Demo Information]. (B) Inherit from DemoBase classes To make your object a "Demo Entity", you must inherit from one of the following base classes depending on your preferred asynchronous style: AsyncDemoBase: Use this for async Task based implementation. CoroutineDemoBase: Use this for standard Unity Coroutine based implementation. 📄 Sample Code 1: Implementing with AsyncDemoBase using System.Threading; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Chobitech.DemoKit; using UnityEngine; public class MyAsyncDemo : AsyncDemoBase { [SerializeField] private float durationSec = 2f; private Transform selfTransform; protected override void Awake() { base.Awake(); // Caching the Transform of this object selfTransform = transform; } // Will execute when demo canceled. public override void OnDemoCanceled(CancellationToken token) { AddLogLnWithColorTag("error", $"{IndividualDemoInfo.name} canceled by {token}"); } // Will execute when demo completed. public override void OnDemoCompleted() { AddLogLnWithColorTag("notice", $"{IndividualDemoInfo.name} finished."); } public override async Task DemoProcessAsync(CancellationToken token) { // Display the start log with "notice" color tag. AddLogLnWithColorTag("notice", $"Start {IndividualDemoInfo.name}"); // Check if cancel is requested. token.ThrowIfCancellationRequested(); /* Horizontal moving */ var initPos = selfTransform.localPosition; var elapsedSec = 0f; while (elapsedSec < durationSec) { token.ThrowIfCancellationRequested(); var x = 2 * Mathf.Sin(2 * Mathf.PI * elapsedSec / durationSec); var pos = new Vector3(initPos.x + x, initPos.y, initPos.z); selfTransform.localPosition = pos; // Display the x of current position on the log area AddLogLn($"x = {x}"); elapsedSec += Time.deltaTime; token.ThrowIfCancellationRequested(); await Task.Yield(); } // Reset position selfTransform.localPosition = initPos; token.ThrowIfCancellationRequested(); } } 📄 Sample Code 2: Implementing with CoroutineDemoBase using System.Collections; using Chobitech.DemoKit; using UnityEngine; public class MyCoroutineDemo : CoroutineDemoBase { private Transform selfTransform; [SerializeField] private float durationSec = 2f; protected override void Awake() { base.Awake(); // Caching the Transform of this object selfTransform = transform; } // Execute on demo canceled. public override void OnDemoCanceled() { AddLogLnWithColorTag("error", $"{IndividualDemoInfo.name} is canceled."); } // Execute on demo completed. public override void OnDemoCompleted() { AddLogLnWithColorTag("warning", $"{IndividualDemoInfo.name} finished."); } public override IEnumerator DemoRoutine() { // Display the start log with "notice" color tag. AddLogLnWithColorTag("warning", $"Start {IndividualDemoInfo.name}"); /* Vertical move */ var initPos = selfTransform.localPosition; var elapsedSec = 0f; while (elapsedSec < durationSec) { var y = 2 * Mathf.Sin(2 * Mathf.PI * elapsedSec / durationSec); var pos = new Vector3(initPos.x, initPos.y + y, initPos.z); selfTransform.localPosition = pos; // Display the y of current position on the log area AddLogLn($"y = {y}"); elapsedSec += Time.deltaTime; yield return null; } // Reset position selfTransform.localPosition = initPos; } } Tip Lifecycle Hooks You can override OnDemoCompleted() to trigger success actions, or OnDemoCanceled() to handle cleanup when a demo is stopped by the user in each DemoBase inherited class. (C) Register with Demo Container Demo Objects in Demo Container Place your created demo objects under the Demo Container GameObject in the DemoMainScene. The system automatically detects and manages objects registered here. Repeat the above steps (A) through (C) for each demo you want to implement. Important Store All Assets in the Workspace Folder To ensure your demo functions correctly after distribution, all related assets—including the DemoMainScene, GlobalDemoInfo, and any custom scripts or models—must be stored within the designated Your Workspace Folder. 3. Distribution Setup Distribution Setup Section Now, you can generate the files for distribution your demo to users with the following steps: Step 1: Select the distribution folder The distribution files will be generated in the selected folder. Step 2: Check the distribution option settings When the option "Remove EventSystem object from the scene" is checked, the system will remove automatically the GameObject attached EventSystem from the DemoMainScene in generating the distribution files. Important If the DemoMainScene includes the EventSystem GameObject, it may be caused compatibility issue or errors depending on user's environment. Step 3: Generate Distribution Files Press the "Generate Distribution Files" button and generate the distribution files. The distribution files will be generated in the distribution folder selected at Step 1. The generated files are: DemoAutoInitialized.cs: A C# script initializes your demo in the distribution destination. DemoAssets.zip: A zip archive contains the files in the workspace folder. DemoKit_Internal.unitypackage: A Unity package file contains the code Chobitech.DemoKit components. Note The following files and folders are NOT CONTAINED in DemoAsset.zip: The system files generated by OS (e.g., Thumbs.db, .DS_Store, etc.) Starting with dot (.) Step 4: Distribute Your Demo Finally, distribute all the generated files as they are to the destination you want to. Caution Do not modify or rename the output files To ensure the demo functions correctly in the destination environment: Do not delete or separate the generated files from the folder. Do not rename any of the generated files. Do not modify or edit the content of the generated files. References API Reference Return to Home © 2026 chobitech. Licensed under the MIT License.

index.html.title: Chobitech.DemoKit | Chobitech.DemoKit
index.html.summary: Chobitech.DemoKit Chobitech.DemoKit simplifies your demo creation workflow through intuitive wizards and ready-to-use tools. Key Features One-Stop Wizard: A comprehensive wizard that handles everything from demo initialization to distribution file generation. Dual Workflow Support: Seamlessly manage both Asynchronous (Task-based) and Coroutine-based demonstration logic with ease. Ready-to-Use UI: Includes a polished header, footer, description area, and a rich-text supported logging system for immediate use. Smart Execution Management: Built-in support for CancellationToken and lifecycle hooks such as OnDemoCompleted and OnDemoCanceled. Clean Architecture: Efficiently manage multiple demo cases within a single main scene, keeping your project organized and clutter-free. Installation via Asset Store chobitech Asset Store page: Please add Chobitech.DemoKit to My Assets and import your project. via Unity Package Download the latest .unitypackage from GitHub Release. Drag and drop the downloaded file into your Unity Project, or import it via the menu [Assets] > [Import Package] > [Custom Package] and select the .unitypackage file. Getting Started Follow our Getting Started guide to build your demo. Also, check the API Reference for advanced customization. Dependencies Unity.TextMeshPro Change Log CHANGELOG Licenses Chobitech.DemoKit is licensed under the MIT License. © 2026 chobitech. Licensed under the MIT License.
